title: Double Outdoor Chaise Lounger
summary: A two-person reclining lounger reusing the same hinge-prop-and-stop-block backrest mechanism as this catalog's single chaise, widened to a shared deck for two — not just the same design copied.
description:
The deck is one wide 2x4 frame decked in 1x4 boards, sized for two people to lounge side by side rather than two separate chairs pushed together. Build the frame first with the longer screws and exterior glue, attach the legs keeping the back rest bay clear, then add 2x2 cleats inside that bay flush to the bottom — these are what the hinged backrest mechanism will attach to later.

**The reclining mechanism is the same hinge-prop-stop-block system as this catalog's single chaise lounge**, just built at double width to support two backrests side by side: 2x2 pieces hinged to 2x4 prop-up spacers let the whole backrest kick up and rest against stop blocks at whatever angle you've positioned them. Build the two back pieces with their own 1x4 slats attached to 2x2 backrest supports, then attach the prop-ups before hinging the entire assembly to the main frame.

**Position the stop blocks only after test-fitting the backrest closed** — the plan is explicit that you need to confirm the back can fully close with the blocks in place before committing to their final position. A generous supply of roughly 16"-long 1x4 offcuts is left over from this build, which is exactly the material this catalog's Adirondack side tables and footstool are sized around, if you want a matching set.

materials.name: Exterior penetrating wood stain or paint

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Build the frame from the sides and front/back pieces using the longer screws, two per joint, with exterior wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs with the longer screws, keeping the back rest bay clear of legs.

steps.title: Add the backrest cleats
steps.body: Attach the 2x2 cleats inside the back rest area, flush to the bottom, with the longer screws and glue.

steps.title: Deck the frame
steps.body: Adjust the frame for square. Glue and attach the long deck pieces to the top with the shorter screws, then space out and attach the center deck pieces.

steps.title: Build the back pieces
steps.body: Attach the back slats to the backrest supports with the shorter screws. Attach the prop-up spacers to the backrest supports with the longer screws, then hinge the remaining prop-up piece to the spacers.

steps.title: Attach the prop-ups to the backs
steps.body: Attach the prop-up assemblies to the back pieces with the shorter screws.

steps.title: Hinge the backrests to the frame
steps.body: Hinge the entire back rest assemblies to the main frame.

steps.title: Position the stop blocks
steps.body: Test-fit the backrests closed, then position the stop blocks inside the frame at the desired reclining angles and attach with the longer screws.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply exterior stain or paint.
materials: Exterior penetrating wood stain or paint

images.alt: Double outdoor chaise lounger with reclining backrests
